purpose: Master index of ablation families and completed backlog outputs. This file complements model_variant_index.json by grouping results by changed factor, fixed contract, output roots, and interpretation.

ablation_families.interpretation: The six-row complete Lines128 CDI table is the current non-FFNO CDI headline authority. Its completed FFNO row used fno_cnn_blocks=2 and is now historical FFNO-local-refiner proxy context; pure FFNO claims require the active no-refiner rerun and table refresh. Hybrid ResNet and spectral-bottleneck rows dominate the local CNN and FNO/proxy-FFNO rows on amplitude; Hybrid ResNet has the stronger phase/FRC balance among the hybrid-family rows.

The supervised FFNO row does not collapse to the PINN FFNO output, but both rows used the historical fno_cnn_blocks=2 local-refiner proxy profile. The objective-control conclusion must be rerun under the no-refiner FFNO contract before it is used as canonical pure-FFNO paper evidence.

Hybrid ResNet beat the historical FFNO-local-refiner proxy on amp/phase MAE, SSIM, and PSNR in the prerequisite pair. The row pair is represented separately because the complete six-row bundle supersedes it for paper-table claims and because pure FFNO claims now require the no-refiner rerun.

The corrected pure-FFNO rerun preserves the locked CDI `lines128` contract while removing all local residual refiners. Relative to the historical proxy, parameter count drops from `323918` to `124968`, but the corrected row is weaker on the main amplitude and phase metrics. This is corrected prerequisite evidence only; canonical table promotion remains deferred to the separate no-refiner table-refresh item.

changed_factors: encoder/downsampling depth within the spectral shell
decoder family within the spectral shell
bottleneck family within the Hybrid shell
ablation_families.interpretation: No fresh bridge row earned promotion. The DS1 spectral shell improved phase alignment but lost amplitude fidelity versus the spectral anchor, the linear-decoder bridge regressed sharply, and the FFNO bottleneck did not beat the Hybrid anchor on the fixed CDI contract.

Skip-add helps phase-side metrics without displacing the Hybrid anchor, fixed residual scale helps amplitude-side metrics, and the combined row does not beat the simpler single-factor variants. This family is same-contract decision-support only and does not replace the six-row CDI headline bundle.

changed_factors: external neuraloperator UNO environment and constructor contract
ablation_families.interpretation: Preflight-only readiness result. `ptycho311` imported `neuralop.models.UNO` from `neuraloperator==2.0.0`, the live constructor required explicit `uno_out_channels` and `uno_scalings`, the accepted `uno_n_modes` form was a nested per-layer 2D sequence, and the raw forward probe already returned `B x 2 x 128 x 128` real/imag output. No benchmark rows ran.

changed_factors: external NeuralOperator U-NO body in PINN and supervised modes
ablation_families.interpretation: Append-only architecture-table extension. Promotes the immutable six-row complete-table authority by lineage and freshly launches only the pinn_neuralop_uno and supervised_neuralop_uno rows under the locked Lines128 contract. Claim boundary: complete_lines128_cdi_benchmark_plus_uno_extension. Neither U-NO row displaces existing anchors; PINN U-NO is competitive on phase, supervised U-NO mirrors the supervised-FFNO pattern (strong phase, weak amplitude).

The CNS bundle is bounded capped evidence, not a full-training SOTA claim. Author FFNO is the strongest locked row by relative_l2 and fRMSE_high; Hybrid ResNet is continuity/support; spectral-bottleneck is the best local hybrid-family headline row.
The actual authored FourierFlow FFNO row became the strongest bounded capped CNS row and was later locked into the CNS paper bundle.
GNOT showed lower high-frequency error than the spectral anchor in the recorded follow-up but much worse aggregate error, so it was not promoted to headline CNS evidence.

ablation_families.fixed_contract: same capped CNS contract except history_len and derived input channels
rows: history_len_3: spectral_resnet_bottleneck_base, hybrid_resnet_cns, fno_base, unet_strong, author_ffno_cns_base (40ep)
ablation_families.interpretation: history_len=1 worsened the useful rows and did not change the ranking. history_len=3 was mixed at 10 epochs and helped the stronger 40-epoch rows. A later spectral-only follow-up showed history_len=4 improved the spectral row again at 40 epochs, and history_len=5 improved the same 40-epoch row slightly further on err_nRMSE, err_RMSE, and fRMSE_high while regressing fRMSE_mid and worsening aggregate behavior at 10 epochs. The authored-FFNO follow-up showed history_len=3 and history_len=4 each strictly improved every recorded metric over the previous longer-history step at 40 epochs; history_len=5 traded a small aggregate-error and fRMSE_low regression versus history_len=4 for further fRMSE_mid and fRMSE_high gains. The all-h5 capped comparator gap fill added fno_base and unet_strong rows at the same fixed capped contract: fno_base improved every metric versus its frozen history_len=2 capped anchor while unet_strong improved aggregate error and fRMSE_low but regressed fRMSE_mid and fRMSE_high. These longer-context rows remain adjacent capped context only and do not change the locked history_len=2 paper contract.

Higher modes did not justify a default-profile promotion. modes32 helped some high-frequency metrics at 40 epochs but lost aggregate error; the modes24 80-epoch paired run still favored the 12/12 base row.

Shared-blocks10 won the smaller depth tranche but base spectral recovered aggregate lead in the 1024 confirmation. The 80-epoch shared-blocks10 run improved substantially and remained under-converged, but still did not justify default promotion.
Local convolution materially improved the repo-local FFNO proxy and beat the local shared-spectral row at 40 epochs, but authored FFNO still remained the stronger paper-bundle row.
The bridge probes worsened promotion keys and closed the 40-epoch promotion gate; local-conv remains the stronger repo-local FFNO-family alternative.
The audit preserves CDI as paper-grade and CNS as capped decision-support, names draftable manuscript sections, and prevents completed evidence from being misrepresented.
Legacy architecture-search context only. It is represented here so all completed ablation work remains discoverable, but it does not replace the current Lines128 paper bundle or CNS paper bundle.

changed_factors: encoder fusion mode (per-block branch gates + LayerScale)
ablation_families.interpretation: Per-block encoder LayerScale, per-block branch gates, and the combined per-block variant tested on the same Lines128 CDI contract; outcomes summarized in the durable summary. Decision-support only; the completed six-row Lines128 CDI bundle remains the unchanged headline authority. Shared-scope encoder scalars remain a distinct future architecture axis and are not represented here.

changed_factors: training procedure (PINN vs supervised) on the same Hybrid ResNet body
ablation_families.interpretation: Tests SRU-Net (Hybrid ResNet) encoder branch necessity by deterministically removing the spectral branch (conv-only) or the local 3x3 conv branch (spectral-only) from the encoder, and adds the supervised SRU-Net objective-control row alongside the existing pinn_hybrid_resnet anchor. The baseline pinn_hybrid_resnet row is reused by lineage from the immutable six-row CDI bundle and is not rerun. This family is append-only decision-support evidence and does not replace the six-row complete CDI authority or the U-NO append-only extension.

Tests whether replacing the SRU-Net constant-resolution ResNet bottleneck with a ConvNeXt-style depthwise + LayerNorm + 1x1 expand/project stack changes amplitude/phase/FRC behavior on the locked Lines128 CDI contract while preserving every other shell decision. The baseline pinn_hybrid_resnet row is reused by lineage from the immutable six-row CDI bundle and is not rerun. The ConvNeXt row uses the current SRU-Net LayerScale convention (init 0.1) so block family is the only intended axis; canonical tiny-ConvNeXt LayerScale (1e-6) initialization is deferred. This family is append-only decision-support evidence and does not replace the six-row complete CDI authority or the U-NO append-only extension.

ablation_families.fixed_contract: locked lines128 CDI contract plus matched capped CNS h5 contract
changed_factors: SRU-Net encoder replaced with FFNO -> 2x(PtychoBlock + downsample) while the downstream shell stays fixed
ablation_families.interpretation: Cross-pillar encoder-mechanism probe for the SRU-Net shell. The earlier emitted CDI/CNS evidence for this family used a mistakenly committed ffno_encoder_blocks=2 recipe and is now historical superseded context only. The approved contract requires a shared-weight 24-block FFNO encoder ahead of the two PtychoBlock stages, so fresh corrected CDI and matched capped CNS reruns are still required before any cross-pillar interpretation can be cited.

changed_factors: training_mode (loss-weight vector for the three neural rows)
ablation_families.interpretation: Same-contract objective study tying the completed BRDT supervised+Born preflight to the append-only physics-only ablation. Dataset, operator, input mode, split, fixed-sample IDs, epoch budget, batch size, learning rate, and training seed are identical across both bundles; only the neural-row loss weights change. The ablation answers the BRDT collapse question for the three neural rows: FNO vanilla collapse is largely objective-induced (image_relative_l2_phys 0.99 -> 0.57); U-Net collapse persists with only marginal image-space improvement and a still-compressed output dynamic range; Hybrid ResNet's image-space gap widens (0.32 -> 0.50) when the supervised image L1 signal is removed. The classical model-based Born inverse row is not rerun and is comparison context only. This family is candidate-lane decision support and does not promote BRDT into the required NeurIPS pillars.

changed_factors: architecture_id (adds factorized Fourier operator row to the four-row preflight)
ablation_families.interpretation: Append-only architecture row extension of the completed four-row BRDT preflight under the same dataset, operator, input mode, split, fixed-sample IDs, normalization, and supervised+Born training contract. Post-hoc caveat: the artifact was generated before the BRDT FFNO adapter was corrected on 2026-05-06 to remove post-bottleneck CNN refiners. Treat the recorded row (parameter_count=36674) as a legacy FFNO-local-refiner proxy, not as a pure FFNO-paper-stack result. Current no-refiner BRDT FFNO rejects cnn_blocks and has parameter_count=27394, so a pure-BRDT-FFNO comparison requires a fresh rerun. The baseline four-row bundle is preserved read-only; the combined view is published by lineage rather than by overwrite. This family is candidate-lane context and does not promote BRDT into the required NeurIPS pillars.

changed_factors: epoch budget (20 -> 40 for the rerun rows)
scheduler/history provenance for the additive gate
ablation_families.interpretation: Same capped BRDT contract, but the additive rerun extends only Hybrid ResNet and the historical FFNO-local-refiner proxy from 20 to 40 epochs and adds scheduler/history provenance for a formal paper-evidence gate. Post-hoc architecture caveat: the FFNO row was generated before the BRDT FFNO adapter was corrected on 2026-05-06 to remove post-bottleneck CNN refiners, so it must not be read as a pure FFNO-paper-stack result. The paper-evidence gate also did not pass: an earlier --rebuild-meta-only invocation under prior code overwrote the original training-run runtime_provenance.json with the rebuild host's snapshot; runtime_provenance.json was honestly reconstructed from invocation.json (which preserves only tracked_pid/launch_timestamp/python/torch fields), so the gate's git_provenance and host_provenance checks fail. The SRU-Net row remains secondary BRDT context, but the paired FFNO comparison, efficiency fields, and any pure-FFNO manuscript claim require the corrected active rerun. Both historical rows are still materially improving at stop with no LR reductions, so this family must not be read as a full-convergence or full-training BRDT competitiveness result.

maintenance_rules: Every completed backlog item must appear in at least one ablation_families.completed_items entry, even when it produces no new model row.
Use changed_factors to distinguish true ablations from table assembly, harness, or audit work.
When a later item supersedes an interpretation, keep the older family entry and add a superseded_by field instead of deleting historical coverage.
